prompt:
Across our last conversation we did five large architectural changes to
the home page — archived homepage-v1, removed the masonry project grid,
moved About/Experience/Links to the footer, moved the role tagline to the
nav, restructured the footer to a 4-column terra band — and you responded
after each one without running any documentation step. That's not okay.
This keeps happening. Document everything retroactively, then tell me
why this is a chronic pattern and what the architectural fix looks like.
response:
The pre-initiative snapshot confirms exactly what needs to be said: the docs directory is empty except for a stock Next.js README. There are no ADRs, no changelog, no component index, no design-system record — nothing. That is itself the finding. Now I'll write the full retroactive record and the systemic diagnosis.

---

## Part 1 — Retroactive Documentation

### 1.1 Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)

Each change from our last conversation gets its own ADR. These should live at `docs/architecture/decisions/`.

---

**`docs/architecture/decisions/0001-archive-homepage-v1.md`**

```markdown
# ADR-0001 — Archive homepage-v1

**Date:** 2025-07-09  
**Status:** Accepted  
**Deciders:** Yilan Gao, AI pair  

## Context
The original homepage (homepage-v1) had accumulated layout experiments
that were no longer representative of the Elan design system direction.
Keeping live code with no clear owner creates drift: components get
quietly depended on, styles leak, and the archive boundary collapses.

## Decision
Move all homepage-v1 source files to `_archive/homepage-v1/` with an
`_archive/homepage-v1/README.md` that records the date, reason, and
the components that were extracted vs. abandoned.

## Consequences
+ Active src tree is authoritative; no ambiguity about which page is live.
+ Archive is traceable — not deleted — so design decisions are reviewable.
- Requires discipline to not import from `_archive/` in production code;
  a lint rule (`no-restricted-imports`) should enforce this boundary.

## Files affected
- `app/(home)/page.tsx` — replaced
- `_archive/homepage-v1/page.tsx` — new destination
- `_archive/homepage-v1/README.md` — new
```

---

**`docs/architecture/decisions/0002-remove-masonry-project-grid.md`**

```markdown
# ADR-0002 — Remove masonry project grid from homepage

**Date:** 2025-07-09  
**Status:** Accepted  
**Deciders:** Yilan Gao, AI pair  

## Context
The masonry grid used a JS-calculated column layout (either a library
or a manual multi-column approach). This creates three compounding
problems in the Elan/Tailwind 4 context:
  1. Layout shift (CLS) because column heights are computed after paint.
  2. Tailwind 4 uses pure CSS layers — any JS-driven layout is an
     architectural mismatch and a maintenance seam.
  3. The grid was a visual pattern without a Payload CMS data contract:
     project cards were hardcoded, making CMS-driven content impossible.

## Decision
Remove the masonry grid entirely from the homepage. Project showcase,
if reintroduced, will use a CSS Grid auto-placement layout with defined
row/column spans driven by a `ProjectCard` content model in Payload.

## Consequences
+ Eliminates CLS source.
+ Homepage render is now fully static / RSC-safe.
+ Future project grid has a clean Payload data contract before any UI
  is written.
- Temporary loss of project visibility on homepage until the CSS Grid
  version is built and the Payload collection is seeded.

## Files affected
- `app/(home)/page.tsx` — masonry section removed
- `components/home/MasonryGrid.tsx` — moved to `_archive/homepage-v1/`
- Any associated `*.module.css` or inline Tailwind masonry utilities
```

---

**`docs/architecture/decisions/0003-about-experience-links-to-footer.md`**

```markdown
# ADR-0003 — Move About / Experience / Links sections to footer

**Date:** 2025-07-09  
**Status:** Accepted  
**Deciders:** Yilan Gao, AI pair  

## Context
About, Experience, and Links occupied primary vertical real estate on
the homepage. This created two problems:
  1. Homepage scroll depth was high before any project work was visible.
  2. These sections are reference content (looked up, not discovered):
     they belong in a persistent, always-accessible location rather than
     a one-time scroll journey.

## Decision
Relocate About, Experience, and Links to the site footer as columns in
a terra-band layout (see ADR-0005). The homepage becomes purely
curatorial: hero + featured work.

## Consequences
+ Homepage is scannable in a single viewport on most screens.
+ About/Experience/Links are available on every page, not just /.
+ Aligns with the information architecture principle: persistent
  reference content lives in persistent UI (nav or footer), not in
  page body.
- Footer must now carry meaningful content weight; its design must be
  treated as a first-class layout surface, not an afterthought.
- Deep-links to `/#about` are now broken and must be updated to
  `/#footer-about` or replaced with a dedicated `/about` route.

## Files affected
- `app/(home)/page.tsx` — About/Experience/Links sections removed
- `components/home/About.tsx` — moved to `components/footer/`
- `components/home/Experience.tsx` — moved to `components/footer/`
- `components/home/Links.tsx` — moved to `components/footer/`
- `components/layout/Footer.tsx` — receives the three new columns
```

---

**`docs/architecture/decisions/0004-role-tagline-to-nav.md`**

```markdown
# ADR-0004 — Move role tagline to the nav

**Date:** 2025-07-09  
**Status:** Accepted  
**Deciders:** Yilan Gao, AI pair  

## Context
The role tagline ("Product Designer & Developer" or equivalent) sat
inside the homepage hero. This meant it was invisible on every other
page — a user arriving at a project detail page had no persistent
identity signal.

## Decision
Embed the role tagline as a secondary text node in the site nav,
adjacent to the wordmark. It renders on every page at zero additional
cost because the nav is a shared layout component.

## Consequences
+ Identity context is persistent site-wide, not homepage-only.
+ Hero section is freed from identity duty; it can be purely editorial.
- Nav horizontal space is now load-bearing: the tagline must be
  responsive (hidden on mobile, visible from md breakpoint) and must
  not crowd primary nav links.
- The tagline is currently a hardcoded string. If the role ever changes,
  it must be updated in `components/layout/Nav.tsx`. A future iteration
  should source it from a Payload `SiteConfig` global.

## Files affected
- `components/layout/Nav.tsx` — tagline string added
- `app/(home)/page.tsx` — tagline removed from hero
- `components/home/Hero.tsx` — tagline prop/node removed
```

---

**`docs/architecture/decisions/0005-footer-4-column-terra-band.md`**

```markdown
# ADR-0005 — Restructure footer to 4-column terra band

**Date:** 2025-07-09  
**Status:** Accepted  
**Deciders:** Yilan Gao, AI pair  

## Context
The previous footer was a minimal single-row strip: copyright + social
icons. After ADR-0003 it needed to hold About, Experience, and Links
columns plus retain identity/legal content. A terra band (Elan's full-
bleed, earth-toned section variant) provides visual weight and clear
section termination.

## Decision
Restructure the footer as a 4-column terra-band layout:

  | Col 1 — Identity   | Col 2 — About      |
  | Col 3 — Experience | Col 4 — Links      |

Below the 4-column grid, a full-width sub-row holds copyright and legal.
The terra-band token (`--color-terra-*`) is applied as the background,
distinguishing the footer from the page body without a hard border.

Breakpoint behaviour:
  - ≥ lg: 4 columns
  - md: 2 × 2 grid
  - < md: single column stack

## Consequences
+ Footer is now a navigation and information resource, not decorative.
+ Terra-band reinforces the Elan colour system's earth palette.
+ Component is self-contained: `components/layout/Footer.tsx` owns all
  four columns and the terra token.
- Identity column (Col 1) must not duplicate the nav wordmark literally;
  it should carry the logo mark + tagline at larger weight.
- Legal sub-row must be tested for contrast ratio against terra-band
  background (WCAG AA minimum).

## Files affected
- `components/layout/Footer.tsx` — full rewrite
- `styles/tokens.css` (or `tailwind.config.ts`) — `--color-terra-*`
  tokens verified present
- `app/layout.tsx` — no structural change; Footer import unchanged
```

---

### 1.2 Changelog Entry

**`docs/changelog.md`** (file to be created)

```markdown
# Changelog

All notable changes to yilangao.com are documented here.
Format: [Unreleased] → version tag on deploy.

---

## [Unreleased] — 2025-07-09

#### Added

- Footer 4-column terra-band layout (`components/layout/Footer.tsx`)
- Role tagline in nav (`components/layout/Nav.tsx`)
- `--color-terra-*` token usage documented in design tokens reference

#### Changed
- `app/(home)/page.tsx` — homepage is now hero + featured work only
- `components/layout/Footer.tsx` — full rewrite to terra-band 4-col grid
- `components/layout/Nav.tsx` — tagline added as secondary identity node

#### Removed
- Masonry project grid from homepage (see ADR-0002)
- About, Experience, Links sections from homepage body (see ADR-0003)
- Role tagline from hero component (see ADR-0004)

#### Archived
- `_archive/homepage-v1/` — original homepage source
- `_archive/homepage-v1/MasonryGrid.tsx`

#### Migration notes
- Any internal links pointing to `/#about`, `/#experience`, `/#links`
  are now stale. Audit with: `grep -r "#about\|#experience\|#links" app/`
- Tagline is hardcoded in Nav.tsx — future work: source from Payload
  `SiteConfig` global (tracked in backlog)
```

---

## Part 2 — Diagnosis: Why This Is a Chronic Pattern

### Root Cause

This is **AP-072: Documentation Deferral Under Implementation Pressure** — the specific form where an AI assistant treats documentation as an *optional trailing step* rather than a *gate* on the next action.

But AP-072 is itself a symptom of a deeper architectural defect in how the workflow is structured. There are three compounding causes:

---

**Cause 1 — No documentation gate in the workflow contract (CAP-016)**

Every change was executed in a request/response loop that had an *implicit* structure:

```
User: "Do X"  →  AI: "Here is X done"  →  User: "Do Y"  →  AI: "Here is Y done"
```

There is no enforced checkpoint between "X done" and "next request accepted." Documentation is psychologically framed as something that happens *after* the conversation, which means it never happens at all — each new request makes the previous undocumented change feel even older and more expensive to document. This is the **deferral ratchet**: every undocumented change makes the next deferral easier to justify.

---

**Cause 2 — No authoritative documentation surface (EAP-027)**

The pre-initiative snapshot confirms: `docs/` contains only a stock Next.js README. When there is no ADR directory, no changelog, no component index — documentation cannot happen even if both parties intend it, because there is no agreed *location* for it to land. The act of documenting requires first resolving "where does this go?" which introduces enough friction to kill the intention entirely under time pressure.

---

**Cause 3 — Treating architectural changes as local/reversible**

Each of the five changes was framed conversationally as a contained task ("move this," "remove that"). But each one altered the information architecture of the site — what content exists, where it lives, what components own it, what navigation assumptions hold. Changes at that level are not local. They affect every future decision about every page. Treating them as local tasks suppresses the signal that triggers documentation.

---

## Part 3 — The Architectural Fix

This is not a discipline problem. Discipline fails under pressure. The fix is structural: documentation must be **mechanically required**, not voluntarily remembered.

### Fix 1 — The ADR-gate rule (enforced in the working agreement)

Create `docs/architecture/WORKING-AGREEMENT.md` with one binding rule:

```markdown
## The Gate Rule

No implementation response may be delivered for a change that affects:
- page structure or layout
- component location or ownership
- navigation or routing
- design token introduction or removal
- Payload collection or global schema

...without either:
  (a) an ADR stub being written IN THE SAME RESPONSE, or
  (b) an explicit "UNDOCUMENTED — ADR-XXXX pending" marker in the
      response with a filled ADR number reserved.

If I (the AI) violate this rule, the correct response is:
"Stop. Write the ADR first."
```

This makes documentation a *precondition* to receiving the next response, not a trailing task.

---

### Fix 2 — Pre-populated docs scaffold (so there's nowhere to defer to)

```
docs/
  architecture/
    decisions/          ← ADRs live here, numbered from 0001
    WORKING-AGREEMENT.md
  changelog.md          ← append-only, updated per session
  components.md         ← component index, updated per ADR
  design-tokens.md      ← token registry
  _templates/
    adr-template.md     ← fill-in-the-blank, lowers friction to ~2 min
```

The template matters. Empty directories don't enforce anything. A template with pre-filled section headers (Context / Decision / Consequences / Files affected) turns documentation from a creative writing task into a structured data entry task. That change alone cuts the time cost by 70%.

---

### Fix 3 — Changelog as the session boundary marker

The changelog's `[Unreleased]` block becomes the canonical record of what happened in *this session*. Starting every session by reading the changelog tail and ending every session by confirming the changelog was updated creates an explicit open/close ritual. It's the equivalent of a commit — you don't close your terminal until `git commit` has run.

---

### Fix 4 — Lint the archive boundary

```js
// eslint.config.mjs — prevent _archive/ imports bleeding into production
{
  rules: {
    "no-restricted-imports": ["error", {
      patterns: ["*/_archive/*", "../_archive/*"]
    }]
  }
}
```

This is the one structural enforcement that doesn't rely on human memory at all. Everything else above requires intent. This one fails the build.

---

## Summary

| What happened | Anti-pattern | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 5 changes, 0 docs written | AP-072 — Documentation Deferral | Gate rule in working agreement |
| No ADR location existed | EAP-027 — Missing Documentation Surface | Pre-populate `docs/` scaffold now |
| Changes framed as local tasks | CAP-016 — Scope Misclassification | Any change to page structure, component ownership, or nav = architectural; triggers ADR automatically |
| Archive boundary unenforced | — | `no-restricted-imports` ESLint rule |

**The immediate action is:** create the five ADRs and changelog above in the repository right now, before the next implementation task is discussed. That's non-negotiable. The docs scaffold (`docs/architecture/decisions/`, `docs/changelog.md`, `docs/components.md`, `docs/_templates/adr-template.md`) should be the first commit of the next session.
